fresh_facts.claim: Iran's IRGC seized or attacked at least three commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours, including Greek- and Italian-owned vessels.
The Trump administration is enforcing a blockade of Iranian ports in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea called 'Economic Fury' to cut off trade.
Thousands more US forces are deploying to the Middle East as part of the Trump administration's enforcement of the Iranian port blockade.
US Central Command seized an Iranian-linked tanker over the weekend and the Majestic X oil tanker on Thursday in the Indian Ocean.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated Thursday that the US military can shift from blockade to major combat operations if needed.
IRGC gunboats fired on a container ship and cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, seizing two vessels and confirming a third attack.
Iran shut down the Strait of Hormuz one day after declaring it open, vowing continuation until the US blockade lifts.
The ceasefire between the US and Iran expired on April 22, 2026.
Internal divisions prevented Iran's negotiating team from traveling to Islamabad for talks with the US, according to Iran International.

The Institute for the Study of War identified a serious intra-regime debate between Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who favors diplomacy, and IRGC Commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi, who opposes US negotiations.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: According to Israeli media (April 19), IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi reportedly has direct access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and serves as a key conduit for regime decisions.
On April 18, 2026, Tehran's Revolutionary Guard fired on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz and reimposed a blockade just one day after Iran announced reopening the waterway.

Ghalibaf's push for diplomacy may be motivated by protecting his domestic political position, as he fears both his and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's positions are at risk if the IRGC consolidates control.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: The regime continues managing aftermath from January 2026 protests, described as the most serious domestic unrest the Islamic Republic has faced.
Prediction markets indicate low near-term regime change probability, with April 30 odds at 1% for regime fall, rising to 3% by May 31 and 13.5% by December 31.
The Islamic Republic is escalating campaigns against the diaspora, exploring confiscation of assets held by Iranians in foreign countries and possible citizenship revocation.
Trump sent a delegation to Pakistan on Monday with Iran declining to participate, with another round of talks planned but currently on hold amid retaliations.
President Donald Trump characterized Iran's leadership as divided between 'hardliners' and 'moderates' and claimed the US had 'total control' over the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran retains thousands of missiles and one-way attack drones despite US claims of depletion.
The White House stated Iran's recent ship seizures and blockade actions do not violate the ceasefire agreement.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran's ceasefire expired April 22 as military escalation accelerates: IRGC seized multiple commercial vessels and reimposed Strait of Hormuz blockade within 24 hours, while US enforces 'Economic Fury' blockade with expanded military deployment and threat of major combat operations. Regime faces compounding pressure from hardliner-moderate split, post-January protest aftermath, and diaspora asset confiscation campaigns.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 22-23, 2026, the US-Iran ceasefire expired, the IRGC seized or attacked at least three commercial ships (Greek and Italian-owned vessels) in the Strait of Hormuz and reimposed blockade, the Trump administration launched 'Economic Fury' port blockade, US Central Command seized Iranian-linked tankers, Defense Secretary Hegseth stated readiness for major combat operations, and thousands more US forces deployed to the Middle East.
